---
title: "End-process HHR Assessment Interim Report"
subtitle: "Country and/or location, Year"
author: "Author(s): Eleanore Sternberg, Stephen Poyer"
date: "Report date:`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output: 
  word_document:
    reference_docx: ref-doc.docx
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}

# Libraries
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(flextable)
library(officer)
library(purrr)
library(knitr)
library(janitor)
library(openxlsx)
library(survey)
library(srvyr)

# Visual themes
# flextable theme
my_theme <- function(x, ...) {
    x <- set_table_properties(x, layout = "fixed")
    x <- border_remove(x)
    std_border <- fp_border(width = 1, color = "darkgray")
    x <- border_outer(x, part="all", border = std_border )
    x <- border_inner_h(x, border = std_border, part="all")
    x <- border_inner_v(x, border = std_border, part="all")
    x <- fontsize(x, size = 8, part = "body")
    x <- fontsize(x, size = 9, part="header")
    x <- fontsize(x, size = 7, part="footer")
    x <- set_table_properties(x, layout = "autofit")
    x <- bold(x, part = "header")
    x <- align(x, i = 1, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header")
    x <- set_table_properties(x, layout = "autofit", width = 1)
    x
}

# Set knitr options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      ft.align = "left",
                      fig.width = 6.5,
                      fig.height = 4,
                      dpi = 300)
```

```{r import, include = FALSE}
# This R script generates the data frames that are used for the report. It also exports clean, raw data into a subfolder.
source("4 End-process check and clean.R")

# Set survey design
# For household data
des <- hh %>% 
  as_survey_design(ids = cluster, # cluster ids
                   strata  = district, # weight variable created above
                   weights = wt, # sampling was stratified by district
                   nest = TRUE) # Specify the dataset

# For individual data
des_ind <- indiv %>% 
  as_survey_design(ids = cluster,
                   strata = district,
                   weights = wt,
                   nest = TRUE)

```

&nbsp; <!-- Add a line break -->

This is an adaptable template that provides guidance and content examples for daily reporting on an end-process assessment of household registration ahead of an ITN distribution campaign, following the Alliance for Malaria Prevention (AMP) recommendations. 

The starting point for this R Markdown is a cleaned data set generated by running the R script: *1 End-process check and clean.R*. The output of the R Markdown is a series of graphs and tables, summarising the results of the in-process HHR assessment. 

**The components of this output are**:

- Table 1. Level of campaign coverage and reasons why households did not receive any campaign ITNs, by district
- Table 2. Level of correct campaign coverage and reasons why households did not receive correct number of campaign ITNs, by district
- Table 3. Current household ITN supply, by district
- Table 4. Population-level ITN access, by district
- Table 5a. Use of available ITNs the night before the survey, by district
- Table 5b. Population-level ITN use, by district
- Table 6. Reasons why people did not sleep under an ITN the previous night, by district
- Table 7a. Level of knowledge of distribution date and location, by district
- Table 7b. Sources of information among households with knowledge of distribution date or location, by district
- Table 8a. Level of exposure to SBC messaging from campaign staff, by district
- Table 8b. Recalled SBC messages delivered by campaign staff, by district
- Table 9. Classification of lots based on receipt of correct number of campaign ITNs
- Table 10 Annex. Level of household registration and reasons why households were not registered, by district
- Table 11 Annex. Regional estimates for key indicators

&nbsp; <!-- Add a line break -->

**Filler text to be replaced by user**
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Suspendisse in sem sit amet ipsum aliquam cursus. Phasellus egestas sollicitudin mauris at rhoncus. Mauris egestas placerat nisi, ac dignissim elit porta vitae. Ut euismod ligula felis. In semper nunc at mauris porta dignissim. Nam ligula massa, convallis at tincidunt quis, tempus eget mauris. In hac habitasse platea dictumst. Praesent facilisis laoreet luctus. Curabitur mollis nisl et nunc faucibus aliquet. Integer ac neque eget justo laoreet fermentum in quis sem. Mauris fermentum quis neque in rutrum. Nulla turpis augue, euismod non ante sed, efficitur vulputate magna. Nulla scelerisque sed erat vel commodo.

Quisque dictum vulputate nibh sed molestie. Morbi bibendum laoreet ornare. Aenean non posuere dui. Vestibulum feugiat justo vitae iaculis efficitur. Nullam commodo, risus sit amet hendrerit euismod, nisi tellus fringilla urna, ac vestibulum justo orci et turpis. Proin sit amet porttitor risus. Proin eget pretium tellus. Sed sollicitudin ex vitae pulvinar ultrices. In scelerisque nec dui ut egestas. Class aptent taciti sociosqu ad litora torquent per conubia nostra, per inceptos himenaeos. Praesent commodo purus non tellus lacinia sollicitudin. Nullam nibh augue, porta non ornare et, ullamcorper non sem. Nullam commodo odio eget lorem dignissim, at ornare urna scelerisque.

Ut a ullamcorper ipsum. Aenean in varius dolor. Nam diam nibh, tempus in magna sit amet, iaculis molestie magna. Nam quis maximus nulla, at dapibus neque. Proin quis urna sed sem malesuada tristique in et purus. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Maecenas sit amet odio interdum, convallis nunc sed, pharetra leo. Donec tortor magna, euismod non lobortis quis, porta vitae sapien. Nulla dapibus nunc elementum, lacinia ex quis, mattis ex. Aenean vulputate pretium faucibus. Nam fringilla magna lectus.

\pagebreak

```{r table1}
# Level of campaign coverage and the reasons why households did not receive any campaign ITNs, by district
# Received at least one campaign ITN (netyn = 1)

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("interviewed", "netyn", "nonet", "noitn_nodis", "noitn_noitn", "noitn_forg", "noitn_decl", "noitn_unaw", "noitn_dk", "noitn_oth")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
# Note: households within a district will all have the same district weight so the weight and unweighted estimates will be the same. However, for consistency, use the survey design here as well. 
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_netyn = round(survey_mean(netyn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_nodis = round(survey_mean(noitn_nodis, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_noitn = round(survey_mean(noitn_noitn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_forg = round(survey_mean(noitn_forg, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_decl = round(survey_mean(noitn_decl, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_unaw = round(survey_mean(noitn_unaw, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_dk = round(survey_mean(noitn_dk, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(noitn_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_netyn = round(survey_mean(netyn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_nodis = round(survey_mean(noitn_nodis, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_noitn = round(survey_mean(noitn_noitn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_forg = round(survey_mean(noitn_forg, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_decl = round(survey_mean(noitn_decl, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_unaw = round(survey_mean(noitn_unaw, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_dk = round(survey_mean(noitn_dk, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(noitn_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t1_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t1_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t1 <- bind_rows(t1_region, t1_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, interviewed, netyn, pc_netyn,
         nonet, noitn_nodis, pc_nodis, noitn_noitn, pc_noitn, noitn_forg, pc_forg,
         noitn_decl, pc_decl, noitn_unaw, pc_unaw, noitn_dk, pc_dk, noitn_oth, pc_oth)

# Create flextable
t1 %>% mutate(spacer = "") %>% # Add a spacer
  select(region, district, interviewed, netyn, pc_netyn, spacer, nonet, noitn_nodis, pc_nodis, noitn_noitn, pc_noitn, noitn_forg, pc_forg, noitn_decl, pc_decl, noitn_unaw, pc_unaw, noitn_dk, pc_dk, noitn_oth, pc_oth) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    interviewed = "HHs with data",
                    netyn = "No.",
                    pc_netyn = "%",
                    spacer = "",
                    nonet = "HHs with data",
                    noitn_noitn = "No.",
                    pc_noitn = "%",
                    noitn_nodis = "No.",
                    pc_nodis = "%",
                    noitn_forg = "No.",
                    pc_forg = "%",
                    noitn_decl = "No.",
                    pc_decl = "%",
                    noitn_unaw = "No.",
                    pc_unaw = "%",
                    noitn_dk = "No.",
                    pc_dk = "%",
                    noitn_oth = "No.",
                    pc_oth = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", 
                            "HHs that received at least one campaign ITN", 
                            "", "",
                            "No distribution in district",
                            "Attended but received no ITNs",
                            "Unable/forgot to attend", 
                            "Did not want to attend", 
                            "Did not know distribution was happening", 
                            "Respondent doesn't know reason",
                            "Other reason"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "Receipt of any campaign ITN", "", "Reasons why HHs did not receive any campaign ITN, among eligible cases"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 15)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_netyn", "pc_noitn", "pc_nodis","pc_forg", "pc_decl", "pc_unaw", "pc_dk", "pc_oth"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 1. Level of campaign coverage and reasons why households did not receive any campaign ITNs, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t2"))

```

\pagebreak

```{r table2}
# Level of correct campaign coverage and reasons why households did not receive correct number of campaign ITNs, by district
# Note: calc_itn_correct = 0 if the number of received ITNs are not equal to the number of ITNs assigned by HHR team OR if HHR team visit (visited) was No or Don't Know. This ensures calc_itn_correct is non-missing for all interviewed HHs and can be used for the LQAS classification of lots with N=60

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("interviewed", "calc_itn_correct", "itn_err","mis_ration", "mis_ranout", "mis_recall", "mis_unk", "mis_oth")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_corr = round(survey_mean(calc_itn_correct, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ration = round(survey_mean(mis_ration, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ranout = round(survey_mean(mis_ranout, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_recall = round(survey_mean(mis_recall, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_unk = round(survey_mean(mis_unk, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(mis_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_corr = round(survey_mean(calc_itn_correct, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ration = round(survey_mean(mis_ration, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ranout = round(survey_mean(mis_ranout, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_recall = round(survey_mean(mis_recall, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_unk = round(survey_mean(mis_unk, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(mis_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t2_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t2_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t2 <- bind_rows(t2_region, t2_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, interviewed, calc_itn_correct, pc_corr, itn_err, mis_ration, pc_ration, mis_ranout, pc_ranout, mis_recall, pc_recall, mis_unk, pc_unk, mis_oth, pc_oth)

# Create flextable
t2 %>% mutate(spacer = "") %>% # Add a spacer
  select(region, district, interviewed, calc_itn_correct, pc_corr, spacer, itn_err, mis_ration, pc_ration, mis_ranout, pc_ranout, mis_recall, pc_recall, mis_unk, pc_unk, mis_oth, pc_oth) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    interviewed = "HHs with data",
                    calc_itn_correct = "No.",
                    pc_corr = "%",
                    spacer = "",
                    itn_err = "HHs with data",
                    mis_ration = "No.",
                    pc_ration = "%",
                    mis_ranout = "No.",
                    pc_ranout = "%",
                    mis_recall = "No.",
                    pc_recall = "%",
                    mis_unk = "No.",
                    pc_unk = "%",
                    mis_oth = "No.",
                    pc_oth = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", 
                            "HHs that received correct number of campaign ITNs", 
                            "", "",
                            "Distribution staff rationed ITNs",
                            "Distribution staff ran out of ITNs",
                            "Error in respondent recall of HHR process", 
                            "Unable to determine reason",
                            "Other reason"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "Correct number of campaign ITNs", "", "Reasons why HHs did not receive correct number of campaign ITN, among eligible cases"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 11)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_corr", "pc_ration", "pc_ranout","pc_recall", "pc_unk", "pc_oth"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 2. Level of correct campaign coverage and reasons why households did not receive correct number of campaign ITNs, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t3"))
```

\pagebreak

```{r table3}
# Current household ITN supply, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("interviewed", "netyn", "itn_recvd_n", "citn_n", "ncyn")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_netyn = round(survey_mean(netyn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ncyn = round(survey_mean(ncyn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_citn   = round(survey_ratio(citn_n, itn_recvd_n, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_netyn = round(survey_mean(netyn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ncyn = round(survey_mean(ncyn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_citn   = round(survey_ratio(citn_n, itn_recvd_n, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t3_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t3_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t3 <- bind_rows(t3_region, t3_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, interviewed, netyn, pc_netyn, itn_recvd_n, citn_n, pc_citn, ncyn, pc_ncyn)

# Create flextable
t3 %>% mutate(spacer1 = "",
              spacer2 = "") %>% # Add spacers
  select(region, district, interviewed, netyn, pc_netyn, spacer1, itn_recvd_n, citn_n, pc_citn, spacer2, ncyn, pc_ncyn) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    interviewed = "HHs with data",
                    netyn = "No.",
                    pc_netyn = "%",
                    spacer1 = "",
                    itn_recvd_n = "No.",
                    citn_n = "No.",
                    pc_citn = "%",
                    spacer2 = "",
                    ncyn = "No.",
                    pc_nycn = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", 
                            "HHs that received at least one campaign ITN", 
                            "",
                            "Number of campaign ITNs received",
                            "Campaign ITNs still in household possession",
                            "",
                            "HHs with at least one ITN from another source"), 
                 colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "Receipt of any campaign ITN", "", "Campaign ITNs still in HH possession", "", "Other ITNs"), 
                 colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 3, 1, 2)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional counts are unweighted; regional percentages are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_netyn", "pc_citn", "pc_ncyn"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer1", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer1", width = 0.1) %>%
  bg(j = "spacer2", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer2", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 3. Current household ITN supply, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t4"))
```

\pagebreak

```{r table4}
# Population-level ITN access, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("defacto")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_accall = round(survey_mean(access1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_accc  = round(survey_mean(access2, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_accnc  = round(survey_mean(access3, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_accall = round(survey_mean(access1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_accc  = round(survey_mean(access2, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_accnc  = round(survey_mean(access3, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t4_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t4_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t4 <- bind_rows(t4_region, t4_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, defacto, pc_accall, pc_accc, pc_accnc)

# Create a flextable object
t4 %>% mutate(spacer = "") %>% # add a spacer for flextable
  select(region, district, defacto, spacer, pc_accall, pc_accc, pc_accnc) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  # Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
  # Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    defacto = "Count of de facto population",
                    spacer = "",
                    pc_accall = "%",
                    pc_accc = "%",
                    pc_accnc = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "", "", "Population access to any ITN", "Population access to campaign ITNs", "Population access to ITNs from other sources"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1)) %>%
  add_header_row( values = c("", "", "Population ITN access"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 5)) %>%
  # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages\n",
                         "Population-level ITN access is the percentage of the de facto household population that could sleep under an ITN, assuming each ITN can protect up to two people")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_accall", "pc_accc", "pc_accnc"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(j = "region", align = "left", part = "body") %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
  set_caption(caption = paste("Table 4. Population-level ITN access, by district"), 
              style = "Table Caption", 
              autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t5"))
```

\pagebreak

```{r table5a}
# Use of available ITNs (campaign ITNs and other ITNs), by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("citn_n", "slept_citn", "ncitn_n", "slept_ncitn")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_sleptc = round(survey_ratio(slept_citn, citn_n, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0), # weighted ratios
            pc_sleptnc = round(survey_ratio(slept_ncitn, ncitn_n, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_sleptc   = round(survey_ratio(slept_citn, citn_n, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_sleptnc   = round(survey_ratio(slept_ncitn, ncitn_n, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t5a_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t5a_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t5a <- bind_rows(t5a_region, t5a_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, citn_n, slept_citn, pc_sleptc, ncitn_n, slept_ncitn, pc_sleptnc)

# Create flextable
t5a %>% mutate(spacer = "") %>% # Add spacers
  select(region, district, citn_n, slept_citn, pc_sleptc, spacer, ncitn_n, slept_ncitn, pc_sleptnc) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    citn_n = "Campaign ITNs in households possession",
                    slept_citn = "No.",
                    pc_sleptc = "%",
                    spacer = "",
                    ncitn_n = "Other ITNs in households possession",
                    ncitn_n = "No.",
                    pc_sleptnc = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "",
                            "Campaign ITNs used for sleeping under", 
                            "", "",
                            "Other ITNs used for sleeping under"), 
                 colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "Campaign ITNs", "", "Other ITNs"), 
                 colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 3)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional counts are unweighted; regional percentages are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_sleptc", "pc_sleptnc"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 5a. Use of available ITNs the night before the survey, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t6a"))
```

\pagebreak

```{r table5b}
# Population-level ITN use, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("defacto", "df_itn", "dfu5", "u5_itn", "dfpw", "pw_itn")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_dfuse = round(survey_ratio(df_itn, defacto, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0), # Proportions
            pc_u5use = round(survey_ratio(u5_itn, dfu5, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_pwuse = round(survey_ratio(pw_itn, dfpw, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_dfuse = round(survey_ratio(df_itn, defacto, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0), # Proportions
            pc_u5use = round(survey_ratio(u5_itn, dfu5, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_pwuse = round(survey_ratio(pw_itn, dfpw, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t5b_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t5b_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t5b <- bind_rows(t5b_region, t5b_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, defacto, df_itn, pc_dfuse, dfu5, u5_itn, pc_u5use, dfpw, pw_itn, pc_pwuse)

# Create flextable
t5b %>% mutate(spacer1 = "",
              spacer2 = "") %>% # Add spacers
  select(region, district, defacto, df_itn, pc_dfuse, spacer1, dfu5, u5_itn, pc_u5use, spacer2, dfpw, pw_itn, pc_pwuse) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    defacto = "Count of de facto population",
                    df_itn = "No.",
                    pc_dfuse = "%",
                    spacer1 = "",
                    dfu5 = "Number of children <5 years",
                    u5_itn = "No.",
                    pc_u5use = "%",
                    spacer2 = "",
                    dfpw = "Number of pregnant women",
                    pw_itn = "No.",
                    pc_pwuse = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "",
                            "ITN use the previous night", 
                            "", "",
                            "ITN use the previous night",
                            "", "",
                            "ITN use the previous night"), 
                 colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "Total population", "", "Children under five", "", "Pregnant women"), 
                 colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 3, 1, 3)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional counts are unweighted; regional percentages are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_dfuse", "pc_u5use", "pc_pwuse"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer1", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer1", width = 0.1) %>%
  bg(j = "spacer2", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer2", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 5b. Population-level ITN use, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t6b"))
```

\pagebreak

```{r table6}
# Reasons why people did not sleep under an ITN the previous night, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("nouse", "r_few", "r_smell", "r_hot", "r_nomal", "r_dislike", "r_out", "r_dkn", "r_oth")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- indiv %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- indiv %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des_ind %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_few = round(survey_mean(r_few, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_smell = round(survey_mean(r_smell, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_hot = round(survey_mean(r_hot, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_nomal = round(survey_mean(r_nomal, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_dislike = round(survey_mean(r_dislike, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_out = round(survey_mean(r_out, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_dkn = round(survey_mean(r_dkn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(r_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des_ind %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_few = round(survey_mean(r_few, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_smell = round(survey_mean(r_smell, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_hot = round(survey_mean(r_hot, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_nomal = round(survey_mean(r_nomal, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_dislike = round(survey_mean(r_dislike, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_out = round(survey_mean(r_out, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_dkn = round(survey_mean(r_dkn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(r_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t6_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t6_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t6 <- bind_rows(t6_region, t6_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, nouse,
         r_few, pc_few, r_smell, pc_smell, r_hot, pc_hot, r_nomal, pc_nomal, 
         r_dislike, pc_dislike, r_out, pc_out, r_dkn, pc_dkn, r_oth, pc_oth)

# Create flextable
t6 %>%
  select(region, district, nouse, r_few, pc_few, r_smell, pc_smell, r_hot, pc_hot, r_nomal, pc_nomal, r_dislike, pc_dislike, r_out, pc_out, r_dkn, pc_dkn, r_oth, pc_oth) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    nouse = "Individuals not using an ITN",
                    r_few = "No.",
                    pc_few = "%",
                    r_smell = "No.",
                    pc_smell = "%",
                    r_hot = "No.",
                    pc_hot = "%",
                    r_nomal = "No.",
                    pc_nomal = "%",
                    r_dislike = "No.",
                    pc_dislike = "%",
                    r_out = "No.",
                    pc_out = "%",
                    r_dkn = "No.",
                    pc_dkn = "%",
                    r_oth = "No.",
                    pc_oth = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "",
                            "Insufficient ITNs for all spaces", 
                            "Don't like smell",
                            "Too hot",
                            "No malaria or no mosquitos",
                            "Dislike using an ITN",
                            "Slept outside", 
                            "Respondent DKN reason",
                            "Other reason"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("","", "Reasons why people did not sleep under an ITN the previous night, among eligible cases"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 17)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_few", "pc_smell", "pc_hot","pc_nomal", "pc_dislike", "pc_out", "pc_dkn", "pc_oth"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # No spacer in this table 
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 6. Reasons why people did not sleep under an ITN the previous night, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t7"))
```

```{r fig6, eval = FALSE}

# Bar graph of reasons why people didn't use their net the previous night
# !!! Add barchart here

```

\pagebreak

```{r table7a}
# Level of information about the distribution activities, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("interviewed", "aware_y", "aware_n")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_both = round(survey_mean(aware_y, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_none = round(survey_mean(aware_n, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_both = round(survey_mean(aware_y, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_none = round(survey_mean(aware_n, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t7a_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t7a_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t7a <- bind_rows(t7a_region, t7a_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(
    region, district, interviewed,
    aware_y, pc_both, aware_n, pc_none)

# Create flextable
t7a %>%
  select(region, district, interviewed, aware_y, pc_both, aware_n, pc_none) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    interviewed = "Households with data",
                    aware_y = "No.",
                    pc_both = "%",
                    aware_n = "No.",
                    pc_none = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "",
                            "Aware of date AND location of distirbution", 
                            "Aware of neither date NOR location of distribution"
                            ), colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "Distribution knowledge"), colwidths = c(1,1,5)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_both", "pc_none"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # No spacer in this table 
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 7a. Level of knowledge of distribution date and location, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t8a"))

```

\pagebreak

```{r table7b}
# Sources of information about the distribution activities, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("aware_8b", "src_cam", "src_chw", "src_ann", "src_lea", "src_fam", "src_rad", "src_tv", "src_sms", "src_new", "src_oth")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_cam = round(survey_mean(src_cam, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_chw = round(survey_mean(src_chw, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ann = round(survey_mean(src_ann, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_lea = round(survey_mean(src_lea, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_fam = round(survey_mean(src_fam, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_rad = round(survey_mean(src_rad, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_tv  = round(survey_mean(src_tv, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_sms = round(survey_mean(src_sms, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_new = round(survey_mean(src_new, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(src_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_cam = round(survey_mean(src_cam, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_chw = round(survey_mean(src_chw, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ann = round(survey_mean(src_ann, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_lea = round(survey_mean(src_lea, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_fam = round(survey_mean(src_fam, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_rad = round(survey_mean(src_rad, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_tv  = round(survey_mean(src_tv, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_sms = round(survey_mean(src_sms, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_new = round(survey_mean(src_new, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(src_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t7b_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t7b_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t7b <- bind_rows(t7b_region, t7b_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(
    region, district, aware_8b,
    src_cam, src_chw, src_ann, src_lea, src_fam, src_rad, src_tv, src_sms, src_new, src_oth,
    pc_cam, pc_chw, pc_ann, pc_lea, pc_fam, pc_rad, pc_tv, pc_sms, pc_new, pc_oth)

# Create flextable
t7b %>%
  select(region, district, aware_8b, 
         src_cam, pc_cam, src_chw, pc_chw, src_ann, pc_ann, src_lea, pc_lea, src_fam, pc_fam, src_rad, pc_rad,
         src_tv, pc_tv, src_sms, pc_sms, src_new, pc_new, src_oth, pc_oth) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    aware_8b = "Number of HHs with at least some knowledge of campaign details",
                    src_cam = "No.",
                    pc_cam = "%",
                    src_chw = "No.",
                    pc_chw = "%",
                    src_ann = "No.",
                    pc_ann = "%",
                    src_lea = "No.",
                    pc_lea = "%",
                    src_fam = "No.",
                    pc_fam = "%",
                    src_rad = "No.",
                    pc_rad = "%",
                    src_tv = "No.",
                    pc_tv = "%",
                    src_sms = "No.",
                    pc_sms = "%",
                    src_new = "No.",
                    pc_new = "%",
                    src_oth = "No.",
                    pc_oth = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "",
                            "Campaign staff",
                            "Health worker / CHW",
                            "Town announcer", 
                            "Community or religious leader", 
                            "Family or friends",
                            "Radio",
                            "TV",
                            "SMS",
                            "Newspaper",
                            "Other reason"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("","", "Sources of information about the distribution, among eligible cases"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 21)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Multiple responses were permitted\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_cam", "pc_chw", "pc_ann", "pc_lea", "pc_fam", "pc_rad", "pc_tv", "pc_sms", "pc_new", "pc_oth"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # No spacer in this table 
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 7b. Sources of information among households with knowledge of distribution date or location, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t8b"))
```

```{r fig7, eval = FALSE}

# Bar graph of source of information
# !!! Add barchart here

```

\pagebreak

```{r table8a}
# Level of exposure to SBC messaging from campaign staff, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("sbc_denom", "sbc_y")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_sbc = round(survey_mean(sbc_y, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_sbc = round(survey_mean(sbc_y, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t8a_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t8a_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t8a <- bind_rows(t8a_region, t8a_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(
    region, district, sbc_denom,
    sbc_y, pc_sbc)

# Create flextable
t8a %>%
  select(region, district, sbc_denom, sbc_y, pc_sbc) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    sbc_denom = "Households with data",
                    sbc_y = "No.",
                    pc_sbc = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "",
                            "HH received information about ITNs or malaria"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "SBC exposure"), colwidths = c(1,1,3)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_sbc"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # No spacer in this table 
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 8a. Level of exposure to SBC messaging from campaign staff, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t9a"))
```

\pagebreak

```{r table8b}
# SBC message content among eligible cases, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("sbc_y", "sbc_use", "sbc_care", "sbc_hang", "sbc_sleep", "sbc_prevent", "sbc_repair", "sbc_oth")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_use = round(survey_mean(sbc_use, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_care = round(survey_mean(sbc_care, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_hang = round(survey_mean(sbc_hang, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_sleep = round(survey_mean(sbc_sleep, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_prev = round(survey_mean(sbc_prevent, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_rep = round(survey_mean(sbc_repair, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth  = round(survey_mean(sbc_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_use = round(survey_mean(sbc_use, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_care = round(survey_mean(sbc_care, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_hang = round(survey_mean(sbc_hang, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_sleep = round(survey_mean(sbc_sleep, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_prev = round(survey_mean(sbc_prevent, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_rep = round(survey_mean(sbc_repair, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(sbc_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t8b_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t8b_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t8b <- bind_rows(t8b_region, t8b_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, sbc_y,
    sbc_use, pc_use, sbc_care, pc_care, sbc_hang, pc_hang, sbc_sleep, pc_sleep,
    sbc_prevent, pc_prev, sbc_repair, pc_rep, sbc_oth, pc_oth)

# Create flextable
t8b %>%
  select(region, district, sbc_y,
         sbc_use, pc_use, sbc_care, pc_care, sbc_hang, pc_hang, sbc_sleep, pc_sleep,
         sbc_prevent, pc_prev, sbc_repair, pc_rep, sbc_oth, pc_oth) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    sbc_y = "Number of cases",
                    sbc_use = "No.",
                    pc_use = "%",
                    sbc_care = "No.",
                    pc_care = "%",
                    sbc_hang = "No.",
                    pc_hang = "%",
                    sbc_sleep = "No.",
                    pc_sleep = "%",
                    sbc_prevent = "No.",
                    pc_prev = "%",
                    sbc_repair = "No.",
                    pc_rep = "%",
                    sbc_oth = "No.",
                    pc_oth = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "",
                            "Use your net",
                            "Care for your net",
                            "Hang up your net", 
                            "Sleep under net every night", 
                            "Nets prevent malaria",
                            "Repair your net",
                            "Other message"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("","", "SBC message content, among eligible cases"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 15)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Multiple responses were permitted\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_use", "pc_care", "pc_hang", "pc_sleep", "pc_prev", "pc_rep", "pc_oth"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # No spacer in this table 
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 8b. Recalled SBC messages delivered by campaign staff, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t9b"))

```

\pagebreak

The five key indicators presented in table below are:
- Percentage of households that received at least one campaign ITN
- Percentage of households that received the correct number of campaign ITNs (based on household size and eligibility criteria for the campaign)
- Percentage of the population that has access to an ITN (assuming each ITN can be used by two people)
- Percentage of the population that reported sleeping under an ITN the previous night
- Percentage of children under five that reported sleeping under an ITN the previous night

\pagebreak

```{r lotclass}

# The table presents the formal LQAS classification, followed by lot-level results for the "five key indicators"
# Lot classification is only at lot level and is unweighted
# Formal LQAS classification: receipt of correct number of campaign ITNs when compared to HHR outcomes
# Note: No HHR visit counts as a failure
formal <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(
    across(c(itn_err), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), # itn_err here is just if they got a net they got the wrong number, 
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(classification = case_when(between(itn_err, 0, 3) ~ "Good",
                               between(itn_err, 4, 8) ~ "Uncertain",
                               itn_err > 8 ~ "Inadequate")) %>% 
  select(region, district, itn_err, classification) %>%
  arrange(region, district) 

# Informal classification using the five key indicators
# Variable names: netyn, calc_itn_correct, access1, df_itn, u5_itn
informal <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(
    pc_cnet  = round(mean(netyn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
    pc_corr  = round(mean(calc_itn_correct, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
    pc_accall = round(mean(access1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
    pc_dfuse = round(sum(df_itn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100 / sum(defacto, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    pc_u5use = round(sum(u5_itn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100 / sum(dfu5, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region) 

# Merge formal and informal classifications, then stack region + district
lotc <- left_join(formal, informal, by = c("region", "district"))

# Create a flextable object 
lotc %>% mutate(spacer = "") %>% # Add a spacer
  select(region, district, itn_err, classification, spacer, pc_cnet, pc_corr, pc_accall, pc_dfuse, pc_u5use) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District (lot)",
                    itn_err = "Number of HHs with incorrect ITNs (denom = 60 HHs)",
                    classification = "Classification",
                    spacer = "",
                    pc_cnet = "HHs with at least 1 campaign ITN",
                    pc_corr = "HHs with correct Nb campaign ITNs",
                    pc_accall = "Population access",
                    pc_dfuse = "ITN use, all ages",
                    pc_u5use = "ITN use, <5 yrs") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "LQAS Classification", "", "Five key indicators (%)"), colwidths = c(1, 3, 1, 5)) %>%
  # Fill cells based on classification value
  bg(i = ~ classification == "Good", j = "classification", bg = "#d9ead3") %>%
  bg(i = ~ classification == "Uncertain", j = "classification", bg = "#fff2cc") %>%     
  bg(i = ~ classification == "Inadequate", j = "classification", bg = "#f4cccc") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_cnet >= 90, j = "pc_cnet", color = "green") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_cnet < 90 & pc_cnet > 80, j = "pc_cnet", color = "orange") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_cnet <= 80, j = "pc_cnet", color = "red") %>%
    color(i = ~ pc_corr >= 90, j = "pc_corr", color = "green") %>%
    color(i = ~ pc_corr < 90 & pc_corr > 80, j = "pc_corr", color = "orange") %>%
    color(i = ~ pc_corr <= 80, j = "pc_corr", color = "red") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_accall >= 90, j = "pc_accall", color = "green") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_accall < 90 & pc_accall > 80, j = "pc_accall", color = "orange") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_accall <= 80, j = "pc_accall", color = "red") %>%
    color(i = ~ pc_dfuse >= 90, j = "pc_dfuse", color = "green") %>%
    color(i = ~ pc_dfuse < 90 & pc_dfuse > 80, j = "pc_dfuse", color = "orange") %>%
    color(i = ~ pc_dfuse <= 80, j = "pc_dfuse", color = "red") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_u5use >= 90, j = "pc_u5use", color = "green") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_u5use < 90 & pc_u5use > 80, j = "pc_u5use", color = "orange") %>%
  color(i = ~ pc_u5use <= 80, j = "pc_u5use", color = "red") %>%
  # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Data sources: Participant recall of ITNs received vs participant recall of HHR outcomes\n",
                         sep = "")) %>%
  add_footer_lines(paste("LQAS decision rules (assuming 60 HHs interviewed per lot:\n",
                         "0-3 failures = Good\n",
                         "4-8 failures = Uncertain\n",
                         "9+ failures = Inadequate\n",
                         sep = "")) %>%
  add_footer_lines(paste("Classification categories for five key indicators:\n",
                         "Green >= 90%\n",
                         "Orange >80% to <90%\n",
                         "Red <=80%\n")) %>%  
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_cnet", "pc_corr", "pc_accall", "pc_dfuse", "pc_u5use"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  #align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
  set_caption(caption = paste("Table 9. Classification of lots based on receipt of correct number of campaign ITNs"),
              style = "Table Caption",
              autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t9"))
```  

\pagebreak

## Supplementary tables

```{r table10}
# Level of household registration and reasons why households were not registered, by district

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("interviewed", "yesvisit", "novisit", "nreg_novisit", "nreg_away", "nreg_refuse", "nreg_dkn", "nreg_oth")

# Unweighted counts (district)
unw_district <- hh %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Unweighted counts (regional totals)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Weighted estimates (district)
w_district <- des %>%
  group_by(region, district) %>%
  summarise(pc_yes = round(survey_mean(yesvisit, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_nov = round(survey_mean(nreg_novisit, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_awa = round(survey_mean(nreg_away, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ref = round(survey_mean(nreg_refuse, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_dkn = round(survey_mean(nreg_dkn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(nreg_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (regional totals)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_yes = round(survey_mean(yesvisit, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_nov = round(survey_mean(nreg_novisit, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_awa = round(survey_mean(nreg_away, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_ref = round(survey_mean(nreg_refuse, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_dkn = round(survey_mean(nreg_dkn, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            pc_oth = round(survey_mean(nreg_oth, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(district = "TOTAL") %>%
  relocate(district, .after = region)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack region + district
# District
t10_district <- unw_district %>%
  left_join(w_district, by = c("region", "district"))
# Region
t10_region <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region", "district"))
# Combine district and region
t10 <- bind_rows(t10_region, t10_district) %>%
  arrange(region, district == "TOTAL", district) %>%
  select(region, district, interviewed, yesvisit, pc_yes, novisit, nreg_novisit, pc_nov, nreg_away, pc_awa, nreg_refuse, pc_ref, nreg_dkn, pc_dkn, nreg_oth, pc_oth)

# Create flextable
t10 %>% mutate(spacer = "") %>% # Add a spacer
  select(region, district, interviewed, yesvisit, pc_yes, spacer,
    novisit, nreg_novisit, pc_nov, nreg_away, pc_awa, nreg_refuse, pc_ref, nreg_dkn, pc_dkn, nreg_oth, pc_oth) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by region
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    district = "District",
                    interviewed = "HHs with data",
                    yesvisit = "No.",
                    pc_yes = "%",
                    spacer = "",
                    novisit = "HHs with data",
                    nreg_novisit = "No.",
                    pc_nov = "%",
                    nreg_away = "No.",
                    pc_awa = "%",
                    nreg_refuse = "No.",
                    pc_ref = "%",
                    nreg_dkn = "No.",
                    pc_dkn = "%",
                    nreg_oth = "No.",
                    pc_oth = "%") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "",
                            "HHs visited by HHR team", 
                            "", "",
                            "HHR team did not visit the HH",
                            "HH not present when HHR occured",
                            "HH refused to be registered", 
                            "Respondent does not know reason", 
                            "Other reason"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "district", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", "HHR team visits", "", "Reasons why HHs were not visited, among unvisited cases"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 3, 1, 11)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Lot definition: District\n",
                         "Regional values are weighted averages")) %>%
  # Formatting
  colformat_num(j = c("pc_nov", "pc_awa", "pc_ref","pc_dkn", "pc_oth"), suffix = "%", digit = 1) %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 10 Annex. Level of household registration and reasons why households were not registered, by district"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t1a"))
```

\pagebreak 

```{r table11}
# Regional estimates with 95% confidence intervals

# Set variables that are summarized in this table
vars_counts <- c("interviewed", "defacto")

# Unweighted counts (region)
unw_region <- hh %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(vars_counts), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

# Weighted estimates (region)
w_region <- des %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(pc_cnet = survey_mean(netyn, na.rm = TRUE, vartype = "ci", level = 0.95),
            pc_corr = survey_mean(calc_itn_correct, na.rm = TRUE, vartype = "ci", level = 0.95),
            pc_accall = survey_mean(access1, na.rm = TRUE, vartype = "ci", level = 0.95),
            pc_dfuse = survey_ratio(df_itn, defacto, na.rm = TRUE, vartype = "ci", level = 0.95),
            pc_u5use = survey_ratio(u5_itn, dfu5, na.rm = TRUE, vartype = "ci", level = 0.95),    
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(pc_cnet = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f–%.1f)", pc_cnet*100, pc_cnet_low*100, pc_cnet_upp*100), # format as percent with CI in one cell
         pc_corr = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f–%.1f)", pc_corr*100, pc_corr_low*100, pc_corr_upp*100),
         pc_accall = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f–%.1f)", pc_accall*100, pc_accall_low*100, pc_accall_upp*100),
         pc_dfuse = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f–%.1f)", pc_dfuse*100, pc_dfuse_low*100, pc_dfuse_upp*100),
         pc_u5use = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f–%.1f)", pc_u5use*100, pc_u5use_low*100, pc_u5use_upp*100)) %>%
  select(region, pc_cnet, pc_corr, pc_accall, pc_dfuse, pc_u5use)

# Merge unweighted N with weighted estimates, then stack
t11 <- unw_region %>%
  left_join(w_region, by = c("region"))

# Create flextable
t11 %>% mutate(spacer = "") %>% # Add a spacer
  select(region, interviewed, pc_cnet, pc_corr, spacer, defacto, pc_accall, pc_dfuse, pc_u5use) %>%
  flextable() %>%
# Create grouping by regiond
  merge_v(j = "region") %>%
  valign(j = "region", valign = "top") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 1, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>% # This colors the rows by region 
  bg(i = ~ region == 2, bg = "#e6f7ff", part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = ~ region == 3, bg = "#fff2e6", part = "body") %>%
# Set headers
  set_header_labels(region = "Region",
                    interviewed = "HHs with data",
                    pc_cnet = "% (95% CIs)",
                    pc_corr = "% (95% CIs)",
                    spacer = "",
                    defacto = "Population count",
                    pc_accall = "% (95% CIs)",
                    pc_dfuse = "% (95% CIs)",
                    pc_u5use = "% (95% CIs)")%>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "", 
                            "Received any campaign ITN", "Received correct campaign ITNs",
                            "", "", "ITN access", "ITN use (all)", "ITN use (children <5)"), colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1)) %>%
  merge_v(j = c("region", "interviewed", "n"), part = "header") %>%
  align(part = "header", align = "center") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "Household-level results", "", "Population-level results"), colwidths = c(1, 3, 1, 4)) %>%
 # Footer
  add_footer_lines(paste("Regional values are weighted averages\n",
                         "Standard errors account for the clustered survey design")) %>%
  # Formatting
  align(j = c("pc_cnet", "pc_corr", "pc_accall", "pc_dfuse", "pc_u5use"), align = "center", part = "body") %>%
  align(i = 2, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(i = 3, j = NULL, align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  # Shade the spacer column as the visual break 
  bg(j = "spacer", bg = "#E6E6E6", part = "all") %>%
  width(j = "spacer", width = 0.1) %>%
  my_theme() %>%
  # Set header
set_caption(caption = paste("Table 11 Annex. Regional estimates for key indicators"),
            style = "Table Caption",
            autonum = run_autonum(seq_id = "tab", bkm = "t11"))
```
